route.name: Pacific Coast Highway: Canada to Mexico

sources.title: Cycling FKT - Pacific Coast Highway
sources.notes: Public source page for the Canada-to-Mexico PCH FKT route; its GPX link points to the RideWithGPS route.

sources.title: RideWithGPS - PACIFIC COAST FKT FINAL FULL
sources.notes: Public route page lists 1,657.5 mi and 79,943 ft for the preferred PCH route; direct GPX export is account-gated.

sources.title: Public Google Drive - Pacific Coast Cycling Route
sources.notes: Public folder containing PACIFIC CYCLE ROAD.gpx; used as the downloadable whole-route GPX source.

sources.title: Adventure Cycling - Pacific Coast Route
sources.notes: Reference source for the broader Pacific Coast cycling corridor; not used as a downloadable GPX source.

route.sourceNote: Planning package geometry resolved from routes/pacific-coast-highway-canada-to-mexico.gpx. Direct public PACIFIC CYCLE ROAD.gpx whole-Pacific-coast source copied into the route library; no clipping or artificial routing was added.
route.description: PCH / Full Pacific Coast corridor from the Vancouver/Blaine border area to San Diego, using a public whole-route GPX source.
route.surfaceMix: Mostly paved coastal touring route; road stress, detours, weather, and logistics matter more than technical trail difficulty.
route.bestSeason: Broad-season coastal route outside major winter storm damage, fire/smoke periods, holiday traffic spikes, and extreme wind/fog windows.

resources.water: Frequent coastal services in many sections, but remote coast, Big Sur/Central Coast, and detour stretches need explicit water spacing review.
resources.food: Resupply is generally available in coastal towns, but hours and long-gap planning need verification for the full corridor.
resources.camping: Requires a multi-week campground/lodging sequence across British Columbia/Washington, Oregon, and California coastal corridors.
resources.toiletsShowers: Confirm by campground/service source before riding.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest bike shops before promotion to ride-ready.
resources.longestGap: Measure from resolved geometry before ride-ready promotion.

tips: Keep planning_grade until the full Pacific Coast corridor is broken into rideable itinerary chunks with current road closures, border logistics, overnight reservations, water/service gaps, traffic/shoulder stress, and bailout plan verified.
hazards: Full-corridor highway traffic, shoulder, bridge, and construction-detour review required.
Exact multi-week campground/lodging and reservation sequence unresolved.
Current DOT, Big Sur slide, county road, park, fire, smoke, and storm-damage checks not recorded.

Public whole-route GPX needs line QA before navigation.
